error.signature: READONLY You can't write against a read only replica

verdict.summary: Write command sent to a Redis replica. Application connected to replica instead of primary.

dead_ends.action: Set replica-read-only no on the replica
dead_ends.why_fails: Writes to replica are not replicated to primary; they get overwritten on next sync, causing data loss
Only appropriate during failover. Otherwise you end up with split-brain: two primaries.

workarounds.action: Fix application connection to point to the primary node
workarounds.how: Check connection string: connect to primary host/port, not replica

workarounds.action: Use Redis Sentinel or Cluster for automatic primary discovery
workarounds.how: Sentinel: redis-py SentinelConnectionPool auto-discovers current primary

workarounds.action: Separate read and write connections in application
workarounds.how: Reads from replica, writes to primary. Most Redis clients support this.
